transit_systems.name: TriMet MAX
transit_systems.description: Light rail system serving the Portland metropolitan area (Blue, Green, Orange, Red, Yellow lines)

stations.name: Gateway/NE 99th Ave Transit Center
stations.notes: Major Eastside hub, Red line branch to airport

stations.name: Washington Park
stations.notes: Deepest transit station in North America. Zoo, Rose Garden.

transit_systems.name: Portland Streetcar
transit_systems.description: Urban streetcar connecting NW Portland, Pearl District, Downtown, South Waterfront, and Central Eastside

stations.name: NW 23rd & Marshall
stations.notes: NS Line northern terminus in trendy shopping district

stations.name: OMSI/SE Water
stations.notes: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry

curated_neighborhoods.name: Pearl District
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Former warehouse district, upscale lofts, Powell's Books, art galleries, cobblestone streets

curated_neighborhoods.name: Downtown
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Pioneer Courthouse Square, food carts, historic buildings, Portland Art Museum, near the river

curated_neighborhoods.name: Nob Hill
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Trendy NW 23rd Ave, Victorian homes, chic boutiques, upscale dining, close to Forest Park

curated_neighborhoods.name: Hawthorne
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Bohemian, vintage clothing stores, Bagdad Theater, progressive culture, historic homes

curated_neighborhoods.name: Alberta Arts District
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Murals, eclectic boutiques, Last Thursday art walk, historically Black neighborhood, diverse dining

curated_neighborhoods.name: Mississippi Ave
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Music venues, quirky shops, food cart pods, lively nightlife, historic Boise neighborhood

curated_neighborhoods.name: Division/Clinton
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Restaurant row, Pok Pok legacy, Salt & Straw, dense residential, high walkability

curated_neighborhoods.name: Sellwood-Moreland
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Small-town feel, antique shops, Oaks Amusement Park, family-friendly, riverside

curated_neighborhoods.name: St. Johns
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Peninsula tip, iconic suspension bridge, Cathedral Park, working-class roots, quiet vibe

curated_neighborhoods.name: Lloyd District
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Moda Center, Convention Center, Lloyd Center mall, transit hub, mostly commercial

curated_neighborhoods.name: Central Eastside
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Industrial warehouses turned coffee roasters, breweries, tech offices, night market

curated_neighborhoods.name: Laurelhurst
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Historic planned community, winding streets, beautiful large park, stately homes

curated_neighborhoods.name: South Waterfront
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: New high-rises, OHSU campus access via tram, river pedway, modern and clean

curated_landmarks.name: Powell's City of Books
curated_landmarks.description: World's largest independent bookstore, occupies a full city block, color-coded rooms

curated_landmarks.name: Washington Park
curated_landmarks.description: International Rose Test Garden, Japanese Garden, Oregon Zoo, Hoyt Arboretum

curated_landmarks.name: Pioneer Courthouse Square
curated_landmarks.description: Known as Portland's living room, public square, brick amphitheater, food carts

curated_landmarks.name: Tom McCall Waterfront Park
curated_landmarks.description: Riverfront park, cherry blossoms in spring, site of many festivals, runs along the Willamette River

curated_landmarks.name: St. Johns Bridge
curated_landmarks.description: Gothic steel suspension bridge over the Willamette, Cathedral Park underneath

curated_landmarks.name: Forest Park
curated_landmarks.description: One of the country's largest urban forest reserves, over 80 miles of trails

curated_landmarks.name: Pittock Mansion
curated_landmarks.description: French Renaissance-style château, panoramic views of the city and Mt. Hood

curated_landmarks.name: OMSI
curated_landmarks.description: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry, submarine USS Blueback docked outside

curated_landmarks.name: Portland Art Museum
curated_landmarks.description: Oldest art museum in the Pacific Northwest, Native American art collection

curated_landmarks.name: Bagdad Theater
curated_landmarks.description: McMenamins restored 1920s movie palace, pub and theater

curated_landmarks.name: Mill Ends Park
curated_landmarks.description: World's smallest park, a two-foot circle in a median on Naito Parkway

street_corridors.name: Burnside Street
street_corridors.vibe: Major east-west arterial dividing north and south, Powell's, distinct bridge crossing

street_corridors.name: Hawthorne Boulevard
street_corridors.vibe: Quintessential SE Portland, vintage shopping, dining, laid-back hipster culture

street_corridors.name: Division Street
street_corridors.vibe: Foodie central, dense high-end restaurants, rapid development, very busy

street_corridors.name: Alberta Street
street_corridors.vibe: Arts district, murals, galleries, boutiques, diverse dining

street_corridors.name: Mississippi Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: Music venues, food carts, independent shops, lively nightlife area

street_corridors.name: NW 23rd Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: Upscale boutiques, national brands, fine dining, tree-lined historic street

street_corridors.name: Sandy Boulevard
street_corridors.vibe: Diagonal street cutting through NE, mid-century architecture, Hollywood Theater

inter_city.notes: Scenic route through the Cascades and Northern California
Route south through the Willamette Valley

weather_config.microclimate_note: Frequent drizzle typical in the winter months
